Frequently Asked Questions

Common questions grouped by area. For deeper coverage of any topic, follow the links to the relevant documentation pages.

General

What is Merlin?

Merlin is an open-source business management application for small businesses. Its core feature is AI-assisted supplier invoice processing: you upload a PDF, DOCX, XLSX, or CSV invoice; Merlin extracts the line items, resolves the supplier, suggests GL account allocations, and posts the transaction — with a human review step when the AI's confidence is below the threshold you set. It also handles sales invoices, recurring invoices, clients, payment terms, a chart of accounts, and financial reports.

Is Merlin free?

Yes. Merlin is free and open-source software (FOSS). You can self-host it at no cost. The only running cost is the Anthropic API usage for invoice extraction — you pay Anthropic directly for the Claude API calls Merlin makes on your behalf. The cost per invoice depends on how many tokens the invoice uses and which Claude model tier answers the extraction request.

Do I need to host it myself?

Merlin is designed for self-hosting on a server you control. You run it like any Laravel application: a web server, a database (MariaDB preferred), PHP 8.4, and optionally a queue worker for background processing. See Installation for the full setup steps. NEEDS CONFIRMATION: whether a hosted/SaaS version of Merlin is available or planned.

What does an invoice extraction cost?

Each invoice extraction makes one or two API calls to Claude. The fast model (Haiku) is tried first; the strong model (Sonnet) is only used when the fast result is uncertain or unreconciled. Token usage for each call is logged in LLM Logs so you can monitor costs over time. Anthropic publishes current pricing at their website.

Invoice processing

Which file formats can I upload?

Merlin accepts PDF, DOCX, XLSX, and CSV. The file-type detection step uses the Magika binary to verify the actual file type rather than relying on the file extension, so a misnamed file is caught before processing begins.

How accurate is the AI extraction?

Accuracy varies by invoice complexity and layout. Merlin reports a confidence score (0–1) for each extraction. Invoices below the auto-post confidence threshold (default 0.90) go to the review queue so a person can check the line items before posting. You can raise or lower this threshold in Purchasing Settings.

Does every invoice need human review?

Not necessarily. When the confidence score is above your auto-post threshold and a matching posting rule fires, Merlin can post an invoice automatically without any manual intervention. For recurring invoices from suppliers you have posted before, the pattern-based matching often keeps invoices off the review queue entirely.

What happens when a supplier is not recognised?

The pipeline first tries to match on tax number, then on name similarity. If no match is found it creates a new supplier in pending status. You can then review and approve the pending supplier on the Suppliers page. Future invoices from that supplier will match against the now-approved record.

Can I reprocess a failed invoice?

Yes. Open the invoice on the Purchase Invoices page and use the Reprocess action. This deletes any existing extracted lines and reruns the full pipeline from the beginning. Reprocessing requires the can-reprocess-invoices permission.

Can Merlin pick up invoices from a folder automatically?

Yes. Run php artisan invoices:watch and drop files into the watch folder (configured via INVOICE_WATCH_DIR). The command polls the folder every INVOICE_WATCH_INTERVAL seconds and ingests any new files it finds. See Configuration — Invoice watch folder.

Accounting

Can I change the chart of accounts?

Yes. The seeded chart is a starting point, not a fixed structure. You can add, edit, or deactivate accounts, account groups, and account types on the Chart of Accounts and Account Groups pages. Accounts marked as system accounts have restrictions on deletion — see Chart of Accounts for details.

Does Merlin handle VAT and GST?

Yes. The default tax rate and the label shown for it (VAT, GST, etc.) are both configurable in Purchasing Settings. On purchase invoices, Merlin stores amounts exclusive of tax and derives the tax amount separately. If the supplier's invoice shows VAT-inclusive line totals, Merlin back-calculates the ex-VAT base so the stored gross matches the printed invoice to the cent.

What financial year does Merlin use?

Merlin's financial year is defined by the financial_year_start_month setting in General Settings. Set this to the first month of your fiscal year (for example, 3 for a March–February year). Reports default to the current financial year's date range. See Reports.

Can I use a currency other than South African Rand?

Yes. The base currency and locale are both configurable in General Settings. The defaults are ZAR and en_ZA, but any ISO 4217 currency code is accepted. Invoices in foreign currencies are converted to the base currency using daily exchange rates fetched from ExchangeRate-API (requires EXCHANGERATE_API_KEY in .env).

Billing

How do recurring invoices work?

A recurring invoice is a template — it defines the client, line items, frequency, and billing day. Every day at 06:00, Merlin checks whether any active recurring invoices are due and generates a full sales invoice for each one. If the auto_send flag is set, the generated invoice is emailed to the client automatically. See Recurring Invoices.

How are due dates calculated?

Payment terms define the rule used to calculate a due date from an invoice date. Merlin supports six rules, including "N days after invoice date", "the Nth of the following month", and "first business day of the following month". See Payment Terms for the full list.

How do I email a sales invoice to a client?

Open the invoice on the Sales Invoices page and use the Send action. Merlin renders the Unlayer email template selected in Billing Settings with the invoice details merged in, then sends it via the configured mail driver. The Send action requires the can-send-sales-invoices permission.

Can I customise the invoice email?

Yes. Invoice emails use Unlayer drag-and-drop templates rather than hard-coded Blade views. Go to Emails › Templates to create or edit a template, then select it as the invoice email template in Billing Settings. See Email (NettMail) for the full workflow.

Security and data

Where are uploaded invoice files stored?

Uploaded files are stored via Spatie MediaLibrary on the source_document collection of each Document record. By default this is the storage/app directory on your server — files are never placed in the publicly accessible web root. The file URL is accessible to authorised users through the application, not directly via the web server.

Is there an audit trail?

Yes. Merlin uses spatie/laravel-activitylog to record changes on all key models. Every document status transition is recorded with the user who made the change and the timestamp. You can view the activity log entries for a document directly on the purchase invoice detail panel.

How do I protect my .env file and APP_KEY?

The .env file contains your API keys, database credentials, and the APP_KEY used to encrypt data and sign sessions. Never commit it to version control — the repository's .gitignore excludes it by default. If APP_KEY is ever exposed, rotate it immediately with php artisan key:generate and note that this invalidates all existing encrypted data, cookies, and active sessions. See Installation for the initial key generation step.

How does the permissions system work?

Merlin uses spatie/laravel-permission. Permissions are defined in migrations and are the stable code contract — the application always checks $user->can('permission-name'). Roles are user-configurable in the UI at Settings › Roles; a role is simply a named bundle of permissions. The seeded Administrator role receives all permissions. You can create additional roles with whatever subset of permissions your team members need. See Roles & Permissions for the full permission list.

Can I restrict which users can post invoices?

Yes. The invoice workflow permissions — can-review-invoices, can-authorise-invoices, and can-post-invoices — are separate and can be assigned independently. A user who can review invoices does not automatically have the ability to post them. Assign the right combination to each role to match your approval workflow. See Roles & Permissions — Workflow permissions.
